As officers continue to explore the attic, they also discover a hidden enclosure where Larry had kept a turkey, some ducks, and a goat. Although not obvious at a glance, the living conditions in the attic are deplorable. The most obvious issue is that the attic isn't being heated, and the few lamps Larry has aren't strong enough to keep the reptiles warm. Many of the animals in the attic are also in critical condition. A raccoon is discovered in a cage with both of its legs broken, eventually leading to it being put down due to the severity of these injuries. Larry's geckos are found with shed skin stuck to their limbs, which could easily lead to limbs being lost. Tragically, one of the geckos died before Maddock was able to rescue it, and another was discovered dead and dried up in one of the enclosures. After all was said and done, Maddock saved 113 animals from Larry's house of horrors, with some of the strangest rescues including two alligators, four chinchillas, seven hedgehogs, and one goat. Despite having to handle many odd and threatening creatures like the eel or alligators, the only animal that proved to be a danger was the agitated goat, which kept headbutting officers as it was led out of the house. However, not all the animals could be saved. A chicken and a French bulldog were both deemed too ill and had to be euthanized. On December 14th, 2022, Larry was given two felony mistreatment of animals charges and 10 negligently provide improper animal shelter charges. As part of a plea deal, Larry pled guilty to two misdemeanors and had the rest of his charges dismissed. For his horrific treatment of the animals in his care, Larry was sentenced to six days in the community reintegration center. Additionally, Larry was prohibited from keeping any animals for one year, with an exception being made for two dogs and two birds, which were returned to him by Maddock. As for the animals taken from him, those that survived were able to make recoveries under Maddock's care and are all looking for new homes.
